question.text: Reports that a derivatives exchange must provide to SEBI include:

answer.text: Occasions when the 99% VaR limit has been violated, defaults by broker-members, daily market activity report, and daily market report
Only the annual financial statements and quarterly compliance certificates
Only default reports; market activity reports are voluntary
Only violations above Rs. 1 crore in value need to be reported

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Derivatives exchanges must report to SEBI: (1) 99% VaR limit violations, (2) defaults by broker-members, (3) daily market activity reports, and (4) daily market reports. These ensure SEBI's ongoing surveillance. **The Trap:** ALL four types of reports are mandatory. VaR violations must be reported even if no actual default occurred. **Cross-Exam Context:** Reporting obligations are regulatory compliance facts. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Exchange to SEBI: VaR violations + defaults + daily activity + daily market reports. All mandatory.
